author: Claude Code
content:
## Changes made

### `init.sh`
- Replaced the warning-only behavior when not in a git repo with `git init --quiet` to create one automatically
- This ensures hooks always have a git root to find `.project/`

### `template/.project/hooks/require-issue.sh`
- Added directory-walking fallback when `git rev-parse` fails
- Walks up from `$(pwd)` looking for `.project/` directory
- Only exits 0 (allow-all) if neither git nor `.project/` can be found

### `template/.project/hooks/require-docs.sh`
- Same directory-walking fallback added

### `.claude/hooks/` (this repo)
- Synced both updated hook scripts

## Root cause
Both hooks used `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` and silently allowed everything (`exit 0`) when no git repo existed. The init script only printed a warning but didn't create a repo.
